records.originalTurkish:
AK PARTİ GRUBU ADINA FUAT OKTAY (Ankara) - Sayın Başkan, değerli milletvekilleri; hepinizi, Genel Kurulu sevgi ve saygıyla selamlıyorum.
Ülkemizin güney kara sınırlarına mücavir bölgelerde terör tehdidinin sürüyor olması ve bunun yarattığı risk ve tehditler karşısında Türk Silahlı Kuvvetlerinin gerektiği takdirde sınır ötesi harekât ve müdahalede bulunmak üzere yabancı ülkelere gönderilmesine yönelik tezkerenin süresinin üç yıl daha uzatılmasına yönelik AK PARTİ adına söz almış bulunmaktayım. Bir kez daha Genel Kurulu saygıyla selamlıyorum.
Ülkemizin güneydoğu komşularında maalesef on yıllardır savaşların, dış müdahalelerin ve istikrarsızlıkların yaşandığına
şahit oluyoruz. 1980 yılında başlayan İran-Irak Savaşı'nı, Irak'ın 1990'da Kuveyt'i işgalini, bunu takip eden Körfez Savaşı'nı, 2003 yılında kitle imha silahlarının var olduğu yönündeki gerçek olmayan bahanelerle Irak'ın işgalini, 2011 yılında Suriye'de başlayan iç savaşı buradan bir kez daha hatırlatmak isterim. Birbirlerini takip eden, birbirleriyle bağlantılı hatta birbirlerinin doğrudan sonucu olan bu olayların bölgeye yönelik doğrudan ve dolaylı dış müdahalelerin sonucu olduğu hepimizin malumudur. Güney kara sınırımızdaki komşularımızda yaşanan bu savaş ve istikrarsızlıklar ülkemizi de hedef alan illegal unsurların ortaya çıkmasına ve Irak ve Suriye'de yerleşmelerine de imkân tanımıştır. Nitekim PYD/YPG, SDG ve DEAŞ yukarıda saydığım dış müdahale kaynaklı savaş ve çatışmaların sonucunda sınır aşan terör örgütleri olarak Irak ve Suriye'de yeşerebilecekleri ve buradan ülkemize yönelik terör saldırıları düzenleyebilecekleri alanları ne yazık ki bulabilmişlerdir. Bugün maalesef bölgede istikrarsızlık devam etmektedir. PYD/YPG, SDG bu istikrarsızlıktan yararlanarak Suriye ve Irak'ta kök salmaya çalışmakta, öte yandan bazı DEAŞ unsurları da yeniden aktif hâle gelme gayreti göstermektedir.
KAMURAN TANHAN (Mardin) - Uzaydan mı gitti bunlar? Kendi memleketlerinde yaşıyorlar, uzaydan gitmediler.
FUAT OKTAY (Devamla) - Suriye'de kalıcı barış ve istikrarın henüz tesis edilememesi, terör unsurlarının varlığına ilaveten ülkemizin güneydoğu sınırlarında bir terör koridoru oluşturulması hayalinde
olan bazı ülkelerin çabaları da maalesef devam etmektedir.
Öte yandan, sınırlarımız, terör tehdidiyle birlikte, aynı zamanda, düzensiz göç ve kaçakçılık açısından da baskı altına girebilmektedir. Bu tehditler, ülkemizin ve vatandaşlarımızın huzur ve güvenliğine doğrudan tehdit oluşturduğu gibi, aynı zamanda, sınır komşularımız için de çok ciddi bir tehdit teşkil etmektedir. Bunun da ötesinde, bölgesel barış ve istikrarı bozabilme riskini de taşımaktadır.
Bütün bu gelişmeler karşısında, ülkemize yönelik terör tehdidinin bertaraf edilmesi; Irak ve Suriye'nin toprak bütünlüğünü bozmaya, istikrar ve güvenlik tesisi çabalarını sekteye uğratmaya ve sahada gayrimeşru oldubittiler oluşturmaya yönelik her türlü tehdit ve eylemlere karşı uluslararası hukuktan doğan haklar doğrultusunda gerekli önlemlerin alınması millî güvenliğimiz açısından büyük önem arz etmektedir. Türkiye, bu bilinçle, Suriye ve Irak'ta, gerekli gördüğü durumlarda sınır aşan müdahalelerde bulunmaktan bugüne değin çekinmemiştir. Bu kapsamda, Fırat Kalkanı, Zeytin Dalı, Barış Pınarı gibi harekâtlar ve operasyonlarla Suriye sınırımızdaki önemli bölgelerde tehditlerin bertaraf edilmesini sağlamış ve bu bölgelerde terör unsurlarını temizleyerek de huzur ve istikrarının teminini sağlanmıştır.
Değerli milletvekilleri, Türkiye, demokratik, siyasi yapısı, insani değerleri, gelişen ekonomisi ve
tarihsel sorumluluğuyla bölgesinde barışın, güvenliğin ve istikrarın en büyük, en güçlü teminatıdır. Bu sorumluluk içerisinde, Türkiye, prensiplere dayanan bir dış politika izlemektedir. Bu prensipler, ülkemizin siyasi ve ekonomik temel çıkarlarının korunması, ilerletilmesi ve bu yönde ikili ve çok taraflı uluslararası iş birliğinin geliştirilmesi, kazan kazan temelinde davranılmasıdır. Bu çerçevede, Türkiye, uzak ve yakın tüm komşularıyla çok boyutlu politikalar geliştirmekte, diplomasiyi sonuna kadar kullanmakta, ekonomik ve kültürel iş birliklerini sürdürmektedir. Keza, bugün ülkemiz, dünyanın çeşitli bölgelerindeki çatışmaların sonlandırılmasında ara buluculuğuna en çok güvenilen ülke konumuna gelmiştir. Bu durum, ülkemize uluslararası alanda duyulan saygı ve itibarı net bir şekilde ortaya koymaktadır. Bazı arkadaşlarımız Türkiye'nin dış politikasındaki itibar boyutunu sorguluyorlardı; bugün dışarıdan gelen bir heyetle bir görüşmemiz vardı, sorduğu net soru şuydu bize: "Biz şunu öğrenmek istiyoruz: 'Avrupa'nın hasta adamı' diye ifade edilen bir ülkenin yirmi yıl içerisinde Avrupa'nın en sağlıklı ülkesi, en sağlıklı adamı konumuna gelmesini nasıl başardınız, bunu bize anlatabilir misiniz?"
BERİTAN GÜNEŞ ALTIN (Mardin) - Çok cinsiyetçi ya bu "adam"lık falan, cinsiyetçi.
FUAT OKTAY (Devamla) - Şimdi kendileri Avrupa'nın en hasta adamı konumuna geldiler ve bölge ve küresel konularla ilgili Türkiye'nin itibarı ve Türkiye'nin dış politikasına duyulan saygıyı ifade eden bir soruydu bu. Biz de anlatmaya çalıştık dilimiz döndüğü kadar.
Bugün ülkemiz dünyanın -biraz önce de ifade ettim- çeşitli bölgelerindeki çatışmaların sonlandırılmasında arabuluculuğuna en çok güvenilen ülke konumunda. Bu durum, ülkemize uluslararası alanda duyulan saygı ve itibarı net bir şekilde ortaya koymaktadır; bunu bir kez daha ifade etmek istedim. Türkiye artık dünyada barışın anahtarı konumuna gelmiştir. Ülkemiz insani yardımlar açısından da yine dünyanın en önde gelen ülkeleri arasındadır çünkü Türkiye bulunduğu yerlerde yok eden değildir, ihya ve inşa eden bir ülkedir. Ülkemiz, barıştan, diplomasiden, refahın artırılmasından ve uluslararası iş birliğinden yanadır ancak ülkemizin güvenliğine yönelik tehditler belirdiği zaman sahada yumuşak gücünün yanı sıra sert gücünü kullanmaktan da çekinmemiştir ve çekinmeyecektir; "kadife eldiven içerisinde demir yumruk" diye ifade etmeye çalıştığımız yaklaşım tam da budur. Güney sınırlarımızda hâlen tehditler devam etmekte, bu tehditler zamanlıca ve kararlılıkla sınır ötesi müdahalelerde bulunmayı zorunluluk hâline getirebilmektedir. Bu tür sınır aşan müdahalelerin zamanında ve etkili bir şekilde yapılmaması hem ülkemiz hem Orta Doğu coğrafyası açısından çok büyük ve geri dönüşü olmayan tahribatlara yol açabilmektedir.
Sayın Başkan, değerli milletvekilleri; güneydoğu sınırlarımız boyunca, özellikle Suriye ve Irak'ta, komşu bölgelerdeki güncel gelişmelere de bir miktar değinmek istiyorum.
Yaklaşık on dört yıl devam eden iç savaştan sonra artık ülkemizin de desteklediği Suriye'de yeni bir rejim mevcut. Toprak bütünlüğünü sağlamış, istikrara kavuşmuş müreffeh bir Suriye hem Suriyeliler hem ülkemiz için kritik bir öneme sahiptir. Mevcut Şam yönetimi ülkenin toprak bütünlüğünü ve istikrarını sağlamak için yoğun çaba harcamaktadır ancak bu ülkede istikrar maalesef tam anlamıyla henüz sağlanamamıştır. Suriye'de devam eden istikrarsızlıkta başta İsrail olmak üzere dış güçlerin bu ülkeye gayrimeşru askerî müdahaleleri de önemli bir rol oynamaktadır. PKK'nın Suriye kolu olan PYD/YPG/SDG son on yıldır iç kargaşadan yararlanarak ve yabancı güçlerin de desteğiyle Suriye'nin yaklaşık üçte 1'ini kontrol altında tutmaya devam etmektedir. SDG işgalindeki bölgenin Suriye'nin tarım açısından en verimli topraklarını, su kaynaklarını ve bu ülkenin hidrokarbon ve yer altı kaynaklarını içermekte olması bu terör unsurlarının neden buralarda faaliyet gösterdiklerini net bir şekilde ortaya koymaktadır, tabii bu terör unsurlarını destekleyenlerin de aynı zamanda. SDG işgalindeki bölgenin Suriye'nin kuzey ve doğu hatlarında, gayrimeşru bir silahlı unsurun Şam Hükûmetinin yönetimi dışında fiilî kontrol sağlıyor olması gerek Suriye gerek ülkemiz açısından ciddi bir endişe kaynağıdır. Geçmiş dönemde sınır harekâtlarını gerçekleştiren bir ülke olarak bugün benzeri tehditlere duyarsız
kalmamız düşünülemez. PYD/YPG/SDG'nin Suriye merkezî yönetimine entegre olmaya yönelik adım atmayı ayrılıkçı ve ayrıştırıcı gündemi dolayısıyla reddetmekle ülkede kalıcı istikrarın tesisine yönelik sürecin ilerletilmesini de engellemeye çalıştığı görülmektedir. Suriye'nin kuzeyinde faaliyet gösteren SDG unsurlarının bölgesel maceralara girmek yerine Suriye merkez yönetimiyle bütünleşme sürecini hızlandırmaları büyük önem taşımaktadır. Bu bütünleşme hem Suriye'nin toprak bütünlüğünün yeniden tesis edilmesine hem de tüm bölgenin barış, huzur ve refahına büyük katkı verecektir. SDG'nin dış güçlerin yönlendirmesiyle hareket ederek maceracı bir tavır takınması, bölge halkını uzun vadede büyük bir tehlikeye sürükleyecektir. Tarih göstermiştir ki bölge dışı aktörlere güvenerek bu tür maceralara girenler sonunda yıkım ve hüsranla karşılaşmışlardır. En doğru, en güvenli ve en kalıcı yol üniter bir Suriye'nin devlet yapısı içinde ve meşru egemenlik sınırları içinde kalınmasıdır. Diğer taraftan, Suriye'de mevcut yönetimin beklenti ve gereksinimi doğrultusunda, ülkenin terörle mücadele imkân ve kabiliyetlerinin geliştirilmesi, sivillerin günlük yaşamlarını ve geri dönüşlerini olumsuz etkileyen mayınların temizlenmesi, ülkedeki kimyasal silahların yerlerinin tespiti ve imhası süreçlerinde de ulusal çabalara eşlik eden uluslararası çalışmaların desteklenmesi ihtiyacı bulunmaktadır.
Sayın Başkan, değerli milletvekilleri; bildiğiniz üzere, PKK terör örgütü "terörsüz Türkiye" süreci kapsamında bir süre önce kendisini feshettiğini açıklayarak silah bırakma sürecine girdiğini ilan etmiştir. PKK'nın kendisini feshinin artık söylemin de ötesine geçerek bir an önce fiiliyata dönüşmesi gerekmektedir. Bu süreci zaten ilgili kurumlarımız da yakından takip etmektedir. Zaten süreçle ilgili de Meclisimizde kurulan Komisyon da yine tüm gelişmeleri yine yakından değerlendirmektedir. Burada şunu görmekteyiz ki: Irak ve Suriye'de meşru hükûmetlerin kontrolü dışında kalan, uluslararası meşruiyeti bulunmayan silahlı yapılar bölge halkına sadece baskı ve yoksulluk getirmiştir. Bu yapıların kalıcı hâle gelmesinin önlenmesi elzemdir, komşularımızın egemenliği, toprak bütünlüğü elzemdir.
Sayın Başkan, değerli milletvekilleri; bugün, bölgemizdeki dengeleri değerlendirirken İsrail'in Gazze'de son on yıldır gerçekleştirdiği soykırım, işgal ve yıkım ile bölge ülkelerine yönelik saldırılarını da göz önünde bulundurmalıyız. Orta Doğu halklarının kaderinin İsrail'in yayılmacı politikaları ve haince planlarıyla manipüle edilmesine izin verilmemelidir, veremeyiz. Bölgenin parçalanmasına yol açacak her türlü girişimin karşısında durulması zaruridir ve biz Türkiye olarak net olarak karşısındayız.
Türkiye sınır ötesi operasyonlarını
Birleşmiş Milletler Şartı'nın 51'inci maddesinde tanımlanan meşru müdafaa hakkı çerçevesinde yürütmektedir. Ayrıca, BM Güvenlik Konseyinin terörle mücadeleye ilişkin kararları Türkiye'nin bu operasyonlarını uluslararası hukuk bakımından meşrulaştırmaktadır. Bu kapsamda yapılan sınır ötesi operasyonlar sadece terörist unsurları hedef almakta, sivillerin zarar görmemesi için azami dikkat gösterilmektedir. Türkiye Cumhuriyeti devleti ve Türk Silahlı Kuvvetleri sadece kendi vatandaşlarını değil, aynı zamanda komşu ülkelerdeki sivilleri de korumayı önemli bir insani sorumluluk olarak görmektedir. Bu tehditlerle mücadele etmek Türkiye için büyük önem arz etmektedir. Bu mücadelenin gerçekleştirilebilmesi için zamanında ve etkili bir güçle sınır ötesi müdahalelerde bulunulabilmesi ve uluslararası iş birliklerine gidilmesi hayati bir önceliktir. Bu kadar yoğun çatışmaların olduğu bölgemizde Türkiye güvenli bir liman olarak kalmayı başarmıştır. Bunda Meclisimizin Hükûmete bugüne değin vermiş olduğu yetkiler son derece önemlidir. Bu net duruşun devamı Türkiye'nin bekası açısından da kritiktir.
Gün içerisinde yapılan bazı eleştiriler de vardı. Bunlardan birincisi, emperyalist planların farkındalığıyla alakalı bir eleştiriydi. Türkiye ve Türkiye'deki yönetim, Cumhurbaşkanımızın liderliğinde tüm emperyalist planların farkındadır ve aynı zamanda da karşısında dimdik durmaktadır. Bu emperyalist planların karşısında
dimdik duran bir Türkiye ve dimdik duran bir lider vardır; o da Recep Tayyip Erdoğan'dır siz tanıyamamış olsanız bile. (AK PARTİ sıralarından alkışlar)
YÜKSEL SELÇUK TÜRKOĞLU (Bursa) - Nasıl mesela?
FUAT OKTAY (Devamla) - İkincisi, ABD Büyükelçisiyle ilgili eleştiriler vardı; gerek Dışişleri Bakanlığı gerekse bizlerin girişimleri sonucu kendi mesajını telafi eden başka bir mesajı kısa bir süre içerisinde paylaştığını biliyoruz. Türkiye'ye meydan okumanın da hiçbir büyükelçinin, hiçbir yabancı büyükelçinin haddinin olmadığını buradan ben bir kez daha ifade ederim; kim olursa olsun. (AK PARTİ sıralarından alkışlar) Zannediyorum, CHP kendi iç sorunlarına hapsolduğu için dış politikadaki gelişmeleri takip etmekte biraz zorlanmakta, zorlanıyor anladığımız kadarıyla.
YÜKSEL SELÇUK TÜRKOĞLU (Bursa) - Onların arkasındaki haritaya bir cevap verseydiniz keşke, haritaya.
FUAT OKTAY (Devamla) - Ümit ediyoruz ki bir an önce kendi içindeki sorunları da çözer ve Türkiye'nin çevresinde, bölgede ve küresel boyutta neler olupbittiğiyle de ilgilenmeye başlar; bu, hem kendileri hem Türkiye için çok daha faydalı olacaktır.
Yine, bir başka eleştiri vardı gün içinde; Türkiye'de özellikle dış politikada Türkiye'yi süt dökmüş kedi gibi tanımlamaya çalıştı bir hatibimiz. Arkadaşlar, Türkiye, özellikle dış politikada bunu ifade ediyorum, süt dökmüş kedi gibi değil, kükremiş aslan gibidir. (AK PARTİ sıralarından alkışlar) Eğer dışarıda süt dökmüş kedi ve ağlayanları, onları arıyorsanız da CHP'ye bakmanız yeterli. (AK PARTİ sıralarından alkışlar) O biz değiliz. Bizler
bir şeye karar verdiğimizde o konuda ne herhangi bir yabancı büyükelçinin kapılarında görüş almak için bekleriz ne de herhangi bir yerden izin isteriz, gerektiği zaman gerekeni yaparız; bu kadar net.
TURAN TAŞKIN ÖZER (İstanbul) - Rahip Brunson'ı anlat, Rahip Brunson'u!
FUAT OKTAY (Devamla) - Eylemlerimiz de bunun en net göstergesidir. (AK PARTİ sıralarından alkışlar)
Tezkerenin uzatılma ihtiyacını ve bölgesel istikrarı iktidarın başarısızlığı olarak yorumlayan arkadaşlarımız oldu, zannediyorum böyle yorumlamak da yanlış olur diye düşünüyorum çünkü bölgemizde devam eden istikrarsızlık ve belirsizlikler bölgesel ve küresel gelişmelerin bir uzantısıdır. Türkiye gerek stratejik, taktik ve gerekse operasyonel seviyede aldığı tedbirler sayesinde çevresindeki ateş çemberine rağmen güvenli liman olarak kalmaya devam etmektedir ve edecektir.
(Mikrofon otomatik cihaz tarafından kapatıldı)
BAŞKAN - Buyurun, tamamlayın Sayın Oktay.
FUAT OKTAY (Devamla) - Tamam Başkanım.
Bir başka eleştiri de eleştiri değil de bir ifadeydi, Suriye'de özerk bir yapının olduğuyla alakalıydı. Suriye'de özerk bir yapı yoktur ve Türkiye'nin de böyle bir kabulü yoktur. 10 Mart mutabakatı çerçevesinde de tek devlet, tek ülke, tek ordu ve tek yönetim çerçevesinde bir entegrasyonu kabul etmektedir Türkiye.
Bir başka eleştiri üç yıllık süreyle ilgiliydi. Özellikle bu üç yıllık süre Türkiye'nin bölgedeki istikrarın sağlanmasına dair kararlılığının bir göstergesidir, üç yıllık sürenin kendisi net bir mesajdır.
Wagner'le ilgili bir ifade kullanıldı. "Wagner"vari yapıyı aramak istiyorsanız bunu bizde aramanıza gerek yok,
İsrail'e bakarsanız görürsünüz. "Haganah" adı altında orada bulacaksınız o yapıyı. (AK PARTİ ve MHP sıralarından alkışlar) Oraya ses çıkarmanızı bekleriz cesaretiniz varsa; biz sesimizi çıkarıyoruz.
(Mikrofon otomatik cihaz tarafından kapatıldı)
FUAT OKTAY (Devamla) - Başkanım, bir dakika daha, yoksa...
Ben tekrar AK PARTİ Grubu olarak tezkereye "evet" oyu vereceğimizi ifade ediyor, Genel Kurulu bir kez daha saygıyla, hürmetle selamlıyorum. (AK PARTİ ve MHP sıralarından alkışlar)
records.englishTranslation:
FUAT OKTAY (Ankara) ON BEHALF OF THE AK PARTY GROUP - Mr. President, dear members of parliament; I greet all of you, the General Assembly, with love and respect.
In the face of the ongoing threat of terrorism in the regions adjacent to the southern land borders of our country and the risks and threats posed by this, I have taken a pledge on behalf of the AK Party to extend the period of the memorandum for sending the Turkish Armed Forces to foreign countries for cross-border operations and interventions, if necessary, for another three years. Once again, I respectfully greet the General Assembly.
Unfortunately, there have been wars, foreign interventions and instability in our country's southeastern neighbors for decades.
We are witnessing. I would like to remind you once again of the Iran-Iraq War that started in 1980, Iraq's invasion of Kuwait in 1990, the Gulf War that followed, the invasion of Iraq in 2003 with false excuses about the existence of weapons of mass destruction, and the civil war that started in Syria in 2011. We all know that these events, which follow each other, are linked to each other, and are even the direct result of each other, are the result of direct and indirect foreign interventions in the region. These wars and instabilities in our neighbors on our southern land border have also allowed illegal elements that target our country to emerge and settle in Iraq and Syria. As a matter of fact, as a result of the wars and conflicts caused by foreign intervention that I have mentioned above, PYD/YPG, SDF and DAESH have unfortunately found areas where they can flourish in Iraq and Syria as transnational terrorist organizations and carry out terrorist attacks against our country from there. Unfortunately, instability continues in the region today. PYD/YPG and SDF are trying to take advantage of this instability to take root in Syria and Iraq, while some DAESH elements are also trying to become active again.
KAMURAN TANHAN (Mardin) - Did they go from space? They live in their own hometown, they did not leave space.
FUAT OKTAY (Continue) - The fact that permanent peace and stability has not yet been established in Syria, in addition to the existence of terrorist elements, is the dream of creating a terror corridor on the southeastern borders of our country.
Unfortunately, the efforts of some countries continue.
On the other hand, our borders may come under pressure in terms of irregular migration and smuggling, as well as the threat of terrorism. These threats not only pose a direct threat to the peace and security of our country and citizens, but also pose a very serious threat to our border neighbors. Moreover, it also carries the risk of disrupting regional peace and stability.
In the face of all these developments, eliminating the terrorist threat to our country; It is of great importance for our national security to take the necessary precautions, in line with the rights arising from international law, against all kinds of threats and actions aimed at disrupting the territorial integrity of Iraq and Syria, disrupting efforts to establish stability and security, and creating illegitimate fait accomplis in the field. With this awareness, Türkiye has not hesitated to make cross-border interventions in Syria and Iraq when it deems necessary.

In this context, with operations such as Euphrates Shield, Olive Branch and Peace Spring, we have eliminated threats in important regions on our Syrian border and ensured peace and stability by clearing terrorist elements in these regions.
Dear MPs, Türkiye is a democratic country with its political structure, human values, developing economy and
With its historical responsibility, it is the greatest and strongest guarantee of peace, security and stability in its region. Within this responsibility, Türkiye follows a foreign policy based on principles. These principles are to protect and advance our country's political and economic fundamental interests, to develop bilateral and multilateral international cooperation in this direction, and to act on a win-win basis. In this context, Türkiye develops multidimensional policies with all its near and far neighbors, uses diplomacy to the fullest, and maintains economic and cultural cooperation. Likewise, today our country has become the country whose mediation is most trusted in ending conflicts in various parts of the world. This situation clearly demonstrates the respect and reputation our country has in the international arena. Some of our friends were questioning the reputation aspect of Türkiye's foreign policy; Today we had a meeting with a delegation from abroad, the clear question he asked us was: "We want to know this: Can you tell us how you managed to make a country, which is called the 'sick man of Europe', become the healthiest country and healthiest man in Europe in twenty years?"
BERİTAN GÜNEŞ ALTIN ​​(Mardin) - This "man" thing is very sexist, sexist.
FUAT OKTAY (Continue) - Now they have become the sickest man in Europe, and this was a question that expressed the respect for Türkiye's reputation and Türkiye's foreign policy regarding regional and global issues. We tried to explain as much as we could.
Today, our country is the country whose mediation is most trusted in ending conflicts in various parts of the world - as I have just stated. This situation clearly demonstrates the respect and reputation our country has in the international arena; I wanted to express this once again. Türkiye has now become the key to peace in the world. Our country is also among the leading countries in the world in terms of humanitarian aid, because Türkiye is not a country that destroys where it is located, it is a country that revives and builds. Our country is in favor of peace, diplomacy, increasing prosperity and international cooperation, but when threats to the security of our country appear, it has not and will not hesitate to use its hard power as well as its soft power in the field; This is exactly the approach we try to express as "iron fist in a velvet glove". Threats still continue on our southern borders, and these threats may necessitate timely and determined cross-border interventions. Failure to carry out such cross-border interventions in a timely and effective manner can lead to huge and irreversible damage to both our country and the Middle East geography.

Mr. President, dear MPs; I would also like to touch upon the current developments in the neighboring regions along our southeastern borders, especially in Syria and Iraq.
After nearly fourteen years of civil war, there is now a new regime in Syria, which our country supports. A prosperous Syria that has achieved territorial integrity and stability is of critical importance for both Syrians and our country. The current Damascus administration is making intense efforts to ensure the territorial integrity and stability of the country, but unfortunately stability in this country has not yet been fully achieved. Illegitimate military interventions in this country by external powers, especially Israel, also play an important role in the ongoing instability in Syria. PYD/YPG/SDF, the Syrian branch of the PKK, has been taking advantage of the internal turmoil for the last decade and continues to keep approximately one-third of Syria under control with the support of foreign powers. The fact that the SDF-occupied region includes Syria's most productive agricultural lands, water resources, and hydrocarbon and underground resources of this country clearly reveals why these terrorist elements operate there, as well as those who support these terrorist elements. The fact that an illegitimate armed element outside the government of Damascus maintains de facto control of the SDF-occupied region along the northern and eastern lines of Syria is a serious source of concern for both Syria and our country. As a country that carried out border operations in the past, it is insensitive to similar threats today.
It is unthinkable for us to stay. By refusing to take steps to integrate into the Syrian central government due to its separatist and divisive agenda, it appears that PYD/YPG/SDF is trying to prevent the progress of the process towards establishing permanent stability in the country. It is of great importance that SDF elements operating in northern Syria accelerate their integration process with the Syrian central government instead of engaging in regional adventures. This integration will contribute greatly to both the restoration of Syria's territorial integrity and the peace, tranquility and prosperity of the entire region. The SDF's adventurous attitude, acting under the guidance of external powers, will put the people of the region in great danger in the long run. History has shown that those who embarked on such adventures by relying on actors outside the region eventually encountered destruction and disappointment. The best, safest and most permanent way is to stay within the state structure of a unitary Syria and within the borders of legitimate sovereignty. On the other hand, in line with the expectations and needs of the current administration in Syria, there is a need to support international studies that accompany national efforts in the processes of improving the country's counter-terrorism opportunities and capabilities, clearing mines that negatively affect the daily lives of civilians and their return, and locating and destroying chemical weapons in the country.
Mr. President, dear MPs; As you know, the PKK terrorist organization announced that it had dissolved itself and entered the process of disarmament within the scope of the "terrorist-free Türkiye" process.

The dissolution of the PKK must now go beyond rhetoric and become reality as soon as possible. Our relevant institutions are already following this process closely. The Commission established in our Parliament regarding the process is also closely evaluating all developments. Here we see that the armed structures in Iraq and Syria, which are beyond the control of the legitimate governments and have no international legitimacy, have only brought oppression and poverty to the people of the region. It is essential to prevent these structures from becoming permanent, the sovereignty and territorial integrity of our neighbors are essential.
Mr. President, dear MPs; Today, when evaluating the balances in our region, we must also take into consideration Israel's genocide, occupation and destruction in Gaza and its attacks on regional countries over the last ten years. The fate of the people of the Middle East should not and cannot be allowed to be manipulated by Israel's expansionist policies and treacherous plans. It is essential to stand against any initiative that will lead to the disintegration of the region, and we, as Türkiye, are clearly against it.
Türkiye's cross-border operations
It carries out within the framework of the right of self-defense defined in Article 51 of the United Nations Charter. In addition, the UN Security Council's resolutions on the fight against terrorism legitimize Türkiye's operations in terms of international law. In this context, cross-border operations target only terrorist elements, and utmost care is taken to prevent civilians from being harmed. The state of the Republic of Türkiye and the Turkish Armed Forces consider protecting not only their own citizens but also civilians in neighboring countries as an important humanitarian responsibility. Fighting these threats is of great importance for Türkiye. In order to carry out this struggle, cross-border interventions with timely and effective force and international cooperation are a vital priority. Türkiye has managed to remain a safe haven in our region where there are so many intense conflicts. In this regard, the powers that our Parliament has given to the Government so far are extremely important. The continuation of this clear stance is also critical for Türkiye's survival.
There were also some criticisms made during the day. The first of these was a criticism about awareness of imperialist plans. Türkiye and the administration in Türkiye are aware of all imperialist plans under the leadership of our President and at the same time they stand firm against them. Against these imperialist plans
There is a Türkiye that stands tall and a leader that stands tall; He is also Recep Tayyip Erdoğan, even if you don't recognize him. (Applause from AK PARTY ranks)
YÜKSEL SELÇUK TÜRKOĞLU (Bursa) - How, for example?
FUAT OKTAY (Continue) - Secondly, there were criticisms about the US Ambassador; We know that, as a result of the initiatives of both the Ministry of Foreign Affairs and us, he shared another message in a short time that compensated for his own message. I would like to express once again that no ambassador or foreign ambassador has the right to challenge Türkiye; no matter who.

(Applause from the ranks of the AK PARTY) I think, as we understand, the CHP is having a little difficulty in following the developments in foreign policy because it is trapped in its own internal problems.
YÜKSEL SELÇUK TÜRKOĞLU (Bursa) - I wish you would give an answer to the map behind them, the map.
FUAT OKTAY (Continue) - We hope that he will solve his own problems as soon as possible and start to pay attention to what is happening around Türkiye, in the region and on a global scale; This will be much more beneficial for both themselves and Türkiye.
Again, there was another criticism during the day; One of our orators tried to describe Türkiye, especially in foreign policy, as a cat that had spilled milk. Friends, Türkiye, I mean this especially in foreign policy, is not like a cat that has spilled milk, it is like a roaring lion. (Applause from the ranks of the AK PARTY) If you are looking for spilled milk cats and crying cats outside, just look at CHP. (Applause from AK PARTY ranks) That is not us. us
When we decide on something, we neither wait at the doors of any foreign ambassador to get an opinion on that issue nor ask permission from anywhere, we do what is necessary when necessary; It's that clear.
TURAN TAŞKIN ÖZER (Istanbul) - Tell about Priest Brunson, Priest Brunson!
FUAT OKTAY (Continue) - Our actions are the clearest indicator of this. (Applause from AK PARTY ranks)
We had friends who interpreted the need to extend the mandate and regional stability as the failure of the government, and I think it would be wrong to interpret it that way, because the ongoing instability and uncertainties in our region are an extension of regional and global developments. Türkiye continues and will continue to remain a safe haven despite the ring of fire around it, thanks to the measures it has taken at both strategic, tactical and operational levels.
(The microphone was automatically turned off by the device)
CHAIRMAN - Here, complete it, Mr. Oktay.
FUAT OKTAY (Continuing) - OK, President.
Another criticism, which was not a criticism but a statement, was about the existence of an autonomous structure in Syria. There is no autonomous structure in Syria and Türkiye has no such acceptance. Within the framework of the March 10 agreement, Türkiye accepts an integration within the framework of one state, one country, one army and one administration.
Another criticism was about the three-year period. In particular, this three-year period is an indicator of Türkiye's determination to ensure stability in the region, and the three-year period itself is a clear message.
An expression was used regarding Wagner. If you want to look for a "Wagner"-like structure, you don't need to look for it with us,
If you look at Israel you will see it. You will find that building there under the name "Haganah". (Applause from AK PARTY and MHP ranks) We expect you to speak out there, if you have the courage; We raise our voice.
(The microphone was automatically turned off by the device)
FUAT OKTAY (Continuing) - President, just one more minute, otherwise.
I once again express that, as the AK Party Group, we will vote "yes" to the motion, and I greet the General Assembly once again with respect and respect. (Applause from AK PARTY and MHP ranks)

AK PARTİ GRUBU ADINA FUAT OKTAY (Ankara) - Sayın Başkan, değerli milletvekilleri; öncelikle sizleri saygıyla selamlıyorum.
Bugün 22 Aralık, 1914 yılındaki Sarıkamış Harekâtı'nın başladığı günün yıl dönümü. FUAT OKTAY (Devamla) - Bunun bedelini Libya halkı ödemiştir. Ülkemizse bunlara engel olmuş, Libya halkının yanında yer almış, Libya'nın parçalanmasını engellemiş, Akdeniz'de barış ve istikrarı korumuştur. Libya'yla 2019 yılında imzalanan Güvenlik ve Askerî İş Birliği Mutabakat Muhtırası çerçevesinde bugün Türkiye Libya'ya yönelik eğitim, danışmanlık, insani yardım ve sağlık desteğini de sürdürmektedir yani
Libya'nın ihyası çerçevesinde de Libya'dayız.
(Mikrofon otomatik cihaz tarafından kapatıldı)
BAŞKAN - Sayın Oktay, lütfen tamamlayın.
FUAT OKTAY (Devamla) - Müsaade ederseniz...
Libya'da kurumların birleştirilmesi, devlet kapasitesinin güçlendirilmesi ve özellikle sivil otoriteye bağlı birleşik ve kurumsal bir Libya ordusunun tesis edilmesi önümüzdeki dönemde de en öncelikler arasındadır. Libya'nın birlik ve bütünlüğü korunarak kalıcı siyasi çözüme katkı sunulması, düzensiz ve kitlesel göçün önlenmesi, insani trajedilerin önüne geçilmesi ve millî güvenliğimizi tehdit edebilecek gelişmelere karşı Türkiye'nin menfaatlerinin etkin şekilde korunması büyük önem taşımaya devam etmektedir. Bu amaçla, Libya Tezkeresi'nin 2 Ocak 2026 tarihinden itibaren yirmi dört ay süreyle uzatılması Yüce Meclisimizin takdirine sunulmaktadır. Bizler de AK PARTİ Grubu olarak bu tezkereye olumlu oy kullanacağız, siz değerli milletvekillerimizi de Türkiye'nin çıkarları doğrultusunda olumlu oy kullanmaya davet ediyor, Genel Kurulu saygıyla selamlıyorum. (AK PARTİ ve MHP sıralarından alkışlar)
records.englishTranslation:
FUAT OKTAY (Ankara) ON BEHALF OF THE AK PARTY GROUP - Mr. President, dear members of parliament; First of all, I greet you with respect.
Today, December 22, is the anniversary of the start of the Sarıkamış Operation in 1914. At the beginning of my words, I commemorate with mercy our martyrs who fell to the ground for the sake of our country in Sarıkamış and all our martyrs who sacrificed their lives for the survival of our country, the peace of our nation and the stability of our region, both within our borders and beyond our borders, and I salute our heroic veterans with respect and gratitude.
Mr. President, dear MPs; Every decision taken in this Parliament directly affects not only today but also Türkiye's future. Because the Libya memorandum is not just a matter of military authorization, and especially not a list to be brought to us in a hurry, like a New Year's shopping list; This expresses your blindness in your view of foreign policy and your lack of seriousness in foreign policy.
KAMURAN TANHAN (Mardin) - You are also very successful, mashallah(!)
BURCUGÜL ÇUBUK (İzmir) - Mashallah(!)
FUAT OKTAY (Continue) - Friends, didn't you know two years ago that this memorandum would expire on January 2, 2026? Therefore, this is a meaningless criticism,
I express it as a dimension that is said just for the sake of saying it.
This memorandum; It is the continuation of a strategic state will that reflects Türkiye's historical responsibility, commitment to international law, vision of regional peace and understanding of national security as a whole.
BURCUGÜL ÇUBUK (İzmir) - Strategic depth.
FUAT OKTAY (Continue) - Yesterday, with an approach disconnected from history, geography, geopolitics, economy and energy security, why Türkiye's presence in Libya is of vital importance and why it constitutes a national security issue, "What is Türkiye doing in Libya?" It is meaningless for those who say this to come here today and make judgments. "Where did the blue homeland come from?" It is meaningless for those who say this to come here today and try to lecture us on foreign policy. (Applause from the ranks of the AK Party) The fact that these views are being expressed, especially on behalf of Türkiye's opposition party, is unfortunately a sad event for Türkiye's main opposition. We hope that, as in the previous one, you, as the Republican People's Party, will fulfill the need to make a correction and perhaps apologize to our nation in a different way.
Let me start with it if you want, "What is Türkiye doing in Libya?" incident because today, even though it was not actually said, the same thing was said between the lines. Someone who does not understand Libya, Somalia, the Gulf of Aden or does not look at the map cannot understand why Türkiye is in Somalia. (Applause from the ranks of the AK Party) Those who do not look at where Türkiye is from the border and if
There is no point in explaining Libya, the memorandum, the memorandum or anything else here to those who do not understand what the signing of that memorandum, that is, the Memorandum of Understanding on Maritime Jurisdiction Areas, means for Turkey, but do you know what is painful?

If the parties entrusted with the foreign policy of Türkiye's main opposition party declare this, woe to Türkiye and the opposition!
TAHSİN OCAKLI (Rize) - It has become clear where we can go with a passport in the foreign policy entrusted to you. 110 countries do not accept us. We entrusted foreign policy to you.
FUAT OKTAY (Continued) - We are sad, we are sad, we are really sad, we will talk about these.
If you want, let's take a look at the history. It's not just about today; relations between Türkiye and Libya date back to the early 16th century, all the way back to the reign of Suleiman the Magnificent. "Oh, you just run away to history." Where will we run to, friends? History is our history, we have nowhere to escape. We always said something, "from the past to the past". Those who cannot take the past into account have no shot. We are not running away, you are running away from history, we have never run away from history. (Applause from AK PARTY ranks)
TAHSİN OCAKLI (Rize) - You are increasing the debt, you are only increasing the foreign debt. When you arrived, it was 125 billion, now you have made a debt of 475 billion.
ADEM YILDIRIM (Istanbul) - History is calling us, history.
FUAT OKTAY (Continue) - In fact, we are a government, an alliance that reconciles today's generation with its past and tries to build the future. We are proud of our history, we do not run away, we visit there to learn lessons from there. In that sense, it dates back to the period of Suleiman the Magnificent and has a deep-rooted history of nearly six centuries. Even the existence of some states on the stage of history today is not that old. Therefore - what you said yesterday and implied today on international grounds - "What is Türkiye doing in Libya?" I would like to say this once again to those who say: Neither your age nor your historical awareness is enough to understand this.
(Applause from AK PARTY ranks)
TAHSİN OCAKLI (Rize) - Shame, shame! Is such a conversation possible? Are you in good shape at your age? Is it possible to talk like this?
ALİ MAHİR BAŞARIR (Mersin) - How diplomatic language do you use, how much(!)
FUAT OKTAY (Continue) - We think not with the narrow maps drawn by others, but with the broad horizon drawn by our history, culture and geography of the heart.
Let's remember: About one hundred and fifteen years ago, Libya existed under the same political roof and under the same flag as us until it was invaded. Even Gazi Mustafa Kemal Atatürk's resistance and struggle against imperialism in Tripoli with his comrades is enough to explain with what sense of historical responsibility the Turkish Armed Forces are in Libya today. If you don't understand this, there is no need to explain anything to you.
BURCUGÜL ÇUBUK (İzmir) - We understand, we understand; We understand money laundering!
FUAT OKTAY (Continue) - Türkiye-Libya relations were not interrupted even after Libya gained its independence in 1951.
BURCUGÜL ÇUBUK (İzmir) - We understand how this country is a black money paradise and its black money potential!
CEYDA BÖLÜNMEZ ÇANKIRI (İzmir) - Everyone listened when your orator spoke, listen!
FUAT OKTAY (Continue) - Türkiye has always shared its state experience with Libya; Libya also sided with Türkiye in recent times, especially during the 1974 Cyprus Peace Operation.

These ties still continue strong. The Libya issue is directly related to Türkiye's security, stability and future in the light of historical ties.
BURCUGÜL ÇUBUK (İzmir) - If the gangs were cleared, the black money trade ended, and the drugs ended, we would have peace and security.
FUAT OKTAY (Continue) - I would like to state this clearly: Türkiye's security cannot be ensured only by measures taken within its own land borders. Instabilities in our nearby geography bring with it multi-dimensional threats such as terrorism, irregular migration and organized crime.
BURCUGÜL ÇUBUK (İzmir) - This is the war policy you invented.
FUAT OKTAY (Continue) - Libya is not a distant geography for Türkiye in this context, in fact it is much closer than thought. First of all, Türkiye and Libya are two countries that are neighbors by sea.
So much so that the distance between Antalya's Kaş Port and Libya's Tobruk Port is the same as the distance between Ankara and Gaziantep by land today. Even this geographical fact clearly shows that the developments in Libya are directly linked to Türkiye's security. In short, Libya is proof that a nation that experienced the first resistance against imperialism in Tripoli has not broken away from history; It is an indication of the state mind that started the defense of Anatolia in Africa; It is the area where plans to exclude Türkiye in the Eastern Mediterranean were disrupted. Libya is the geography where our sovereignty rights in the seas are defended on the field as well as at the table; It is a geopolitical point where Türkiye shows that it is a state that is not just watching, but making games; It is a strategic threshold that if we do not exist today, we will pay a much higher price in Libya tomorrow.
Ultimately, our cooperation with Libya is a representation of the will to defend the interests of the brotherly Libyan people, sovereignty in the seas, balance in the region, influence in Africa, legitimacy in the Mediterranean and having a say in the international system.
BURCUGÜL ÇUBUK (İzmir) - And illegitimate trade.
FUAT OKTAY (Continue) - In other words, it is not lack of foresight but foresight, thinking not only about the present but also the future, having a vision is the Libya issue.
Mr. President, dear MPs; As we all know, with the overthrow of the Gaddafi regime in 2011, a serious authority vacuum emerged in Libya and the country was dragged into a long-term spiral of instability. In this process, under the leadership of the United Nations and
As a result of the political dialogue carried out with the active contributions of Türkiye, the Libya Political Agreement was signed on 17 December 2015. The Government of National Accord established with this agreement was recognized as the sole legitimate representative of Libya by the United Nations Security Council Resolution No. 2259. Instead of always looking at the points against Türkiye, as a citizen of Türkiye and a member of its political party, it would be useful to look at the events from Türkiye's perspective, I especially recommend this.
This decision, which constitutes a fundamental reference for the international community regarding Libya, was also confirmed by subsequent Security Council decisions.
BURCUGÜL ÇUBUK (İzmir) - Where did the United Nations bring peace?
FUAT OKTAY (Continue) - The legal basis of Türkiye's approach towards Libya is based on this clear and undisputed legitimacy.

Türkiye relied on legitimacy, institutions and international law in Libya.
BURCUGÜL ÇUBUK (İzmir) - If he were legitimate, he would protect his own government.
FUAT OKTAY (Continue) - As a matter of fact, the legitimate Government of National Accord requested support from the international community in April 2019 against attacks aimed at overthrowing the administration. Based on this call, Türkiye has decided to provide the necessary support within the framework of international law. Our Supreme Assembly has fulfilled its responsibility in this context, and Türkiye has become the only country that responded to the call of the legitimate government in Libya. Türkiye's presence in Libya is an open, transparent and lawful cooperation upon the invitation of the legitimate government. There is nothing being done behind closed doors. "It is a dark scenario." You know very well what you are talking about, black affairs.
You know how the land affairs are carried out and how they are cleaned, we do not. Therefore, if you look in the mirror there, you will see that Türkiye's relations in Libya are transparent, open and comply with international law.
With this support provided by Türkiye, the risk of civil war in Libya has been eliminated, and the security threat posed by DAESH, Al Qaeda and similar terrorist organizations that benefit from instability has been significantly weakened. DAESH and Al Qaeda are not things we put in as side dishes, gentlemen and ladies; This is the reality on the ground.
CEYLAN AKÇA CUPOLO (Diyarbakır) - It's not a side dish, it's a sauce!
FUAT OKTAY (Continue) - And he makes decisions within the framework of the realities on the field, acts with the mind of the state, does not go to bed at night and wake up in the morning, and does not do business under Erdogan's instructions. It is extremely grave for someone who was an ambassador and a representative of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs to say that the life of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs is over and that he is doing things under Erdoğan's instructions. (Applause from AK PARTY ranks)
TAHSİN OCAKLI (Rize) - You can't even take a step! You can't even take a step! None of you can even take a step without instructions!
FUAT OKTAY (Continue) - While you were working at the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, Erdoğan, did President Erdoğan call you and give you instructions? Then explain to us what illegal instructions you received and did.
TURAN TAŞKIN ÖZER (Istanbul) - There is teasing.
FUAT OKTAY (Continue) - If you did it, if you did it, how did you do it as the ambassador of the Republic of Türkiye? Our Supreme Assembly.
TURAN TAŞKIN ÖZER (Istanbul) - The right to reply has arisen.
FUAT OKTAY (Continue) - There is no right of reply, we are asking clearly anyway.
In our Supreme Assembly, again, in this place, everything is done openly and transparently.
ADEM YILDIRIM (Istanbul) - He will answer clearly.
FUAT OKTAY (Continue) - I am a friend of yours who has actually served as the Vice President of the Republic of Türkiye and the President. Ministry of Foreign Affairs.
TURAN TAŞKIN ÖZER (Istanbul) - Formerly.
VEYSAL TİPİOĞLU (Kocaeli) - In the past, in the past. Learn a little Turkish, friend.
FUAT OKTAY (Continuing) - "I'm a friend of yours who did it." I say. I don't know where you were before.
Therefore
It was not possible for our President to make any decision on any issue without taking the opinion of our relevant institutions and examining the incident in detail with all our relevant institutions, and he did not do this.

Unfortunately, this is expressed only by a staff who do not know, who have no experience in government today, and who have forgotten their past experiences. (Applause from the ranks of the AK Party) The nation will not give you this experience anyway - if you go with this mentality.
TURAN TAŞKIN ÖZER (Istanbul) - We will see!
FUAT OKTAY (Continued) - Unfortunately, I find this sad for the opposition.
TURAN TAŞKIN ÖZER (Istanbul) - Mr. President, bring the ballot box, let's see!
SERKAN SARI (Balıkesir) - Citizens should decide this!
FUAT OKTAY (Continue) - With this support provided by Türkiye, a ceasefire environment was established on the field, paving the way for the political process led by the United Nations, whose ultimate goal is elections. The fact that a ceasefire can be discussed in Libya today and that the political process has not been completely broken is a direct result of Türkiye's presence on the field and at the table.
Mr. President, dear MPs; At this point today, the main goal is to successfully complete the political process in Libya and thus establish permanent stability and security in the Mediterranean and North Africa. However, the fact that elections have not been held since December 24, 2021 deepens the political uncertainty in the country and risks the atmosphere of tranquility achieved with great sacrifices in the field. The divided governance structure and the ongoing power struggle keep alive the possibility of Libya being dragged into an armed conflict environment again. This situation has the potential to negatively affect not only Libya, but also the Mediterranean basin, North Africa and naturally Türkiye's interests in the region. In this regard, Türkiye supports the political process led and owned by Libya; fair,
advocates holding free and inclusive Presidential and Parliamentary elections as soon as possible. Türkiye's Libya policy is based on a holistic approach covering the west, east and south of the country. In this context, Türkiye continues its dialogue with all segments of Libya and takes the unity and integrity of the country as a basis.
Mr. President, dear MPs; The Memorandum of Understanding on the Delimitation of Maritime Jurisdiction Areas signed with Libya in 2019 is a legitimate and binding agreement prepared in accordance with international law, aiming to protect the sovereign rights of Türkiye and Libya in the Eastern Mediterranean. According to Libyan legislation, government approval is sufficient for this memorandum to come into force. Therefore, the memorandum of understanding is currently in force and has been registered by both countries with the United Nations. There are countries that have something to do with this, Greece is one of them, the Greek Cypriot side is one of them; I don't understand why you are in pain. (Applause from the ranks of the AK PARTY) I have just stated that according to Libyan legislation, government approval is sufficient for this memorandum to come into force. The process initiated in the House of Representatives developed on the initiative of Libyans who are aware that this agreement serves the interests of Libya and is a clear political message to the parties that attempt to violate Libya's rights at sea.
CEYLAN AKÇA CUPOLO (Diyarbakır) - If there is no election, how do you know what Libya is waiting for?

FUAT OKTAY (Continue) - Mr. President, dear members of parliament; Our cooperation with Libya is legitimate and against the plans that try to confine Türkiye and Libya to the coast in the Eastern Mediterranean.
It is a legal stance. Blue homeland - there were those who objected, there were those who made fun of it, they were acting according to their own opinion, according to their thoughts - is a strategic approach that expresses the protection of Türkiye's sovereign rights in the seas on the basis of international law. If you remember, as I mentioned before, there were those who described the will to defend the rights in the high seas as an "overseas adventure" and the concept of the "blue homeland" as a "fairy tale". Just as it is unacceptable to describe an inch of land from the motherland as an adventure, it is equally unacceptable to belittle our sovereign rights in the blue homeland. The blue homeland is our priority today, just like yesterday. When discussing Türkiye's struggle for sovereignty in the high seas, one should not engage in politics as if speaking from the deck of a ship whose sails are inflated by non-national groups.
TAHSİN OCAKLI (Rize) - They hit 4 of our ships, where were you in the blue homeland? 4 Turkish ships were hit, wasn't the blue homeland a homeland then?
FUAT OKTAY (Continue) - Mr. President, dear members of parliament; Foreign powers, some of whom have been playing games on Libya with their old colonial reflexes since 2010-2011, have unfortunately evaluated Libya as an element of energy interests, regional power projections, ideological goals and strategic bargaining. These countries did not hesitate to turn Libya into a conflict environment.
CEYLAN AKÇA CUPOLO (Diyarbakır) - Why did you allow Russian armed groups then?
FUAT OKTAY (Continued) - The Libyan people paid the price for this. Our country prevented these, stood by the Libyan people, prevented the disintegration of Libya, and preserved peace and stability in the Mediterranean. Within the framework of the Security and Military Cooperation Memorandum of Understanding signed with Libya in 2019, Türkiye continues its education, consultancy, humanitarian aid and health support to Libya.
We are in Libya within the framework of Libya's revival.
(The microphone was automatically turned off by the device)
CHAIRMAN - Mr. Oktay, please complete.
FUAT OKTAY (Continue) - If you allow.
Unifying institutions in Libya, strengthening state capacity, and especially establishing a unified and institutional Libyan army subordinate to civilian authority are among the top priorities in the coming period. It continues to be of great importance to contribute to a permanent political solution by preserving the unity and integrity of Libya, to prevent irregular and mass migration, to prevent humanitarian tragedies, and to effectively protect Türkiye's interests against developments that may threaten our national security. For this purpose, the extension of the Libya Memorandum for twenty-four months, starting from January 2, 2026, is submitted to the discretion of our Supreme Assembly. We, as the AK Party Group, will vote positively for this motion. I invite you, our esteemed MPs, to vote positively in line with Türkiye's interests, and I respectfully greet the General Assembly. (Applause from AK PARTY and MHP ranks)
records.englishTitle: The Presidency's responsibility is to take all necessary measures within the framework of international law against all kinds of threats and security risks to Turkey's national interests, to eliminate attacks that may be directed at Turkey's interests in Libya by illegitimate armed groups and terrorist organizations in Libya, to ensure the maintenance of our national security against other possible risks such as mass migration, to deliver the humanitarian aid needed by the Libyan people, to eliminate the activities of DAESH, Al Qaeda and other terrorist organizations that threaten the integrity and stability of Libya. to eliminate illegal armed groups and actions that create a suitable environment for illegal migration and human trafficking, to use the authority given to member states by the UN Security Council Resolution 2292 (2016) to supervise the arms embargo on Libya on the high seas, if necessary, to comply with the demands of the then Libyan Government of National Accord and the subsequently established National Unity Government.
